layman_headline: Market fear is extreme but no clean entry stands out yet — patience is the play.
layman_explanation: The crypto market is in 'Extreme Fear' territory at 25/100, which historically can present buying opportunities. Bitcoin is bouncing nicely with 5 straight days of ETF inflows, and most major coins are green. However, the swing trade candidates right now are either already extended after big rallies (LDO +25%, ONDO +30% in a week) or still in ugly downtrends (BONK, NIGHT). None offer a clean risk/reward entry at current prices.
layman_action: Wait for a pullback in ETH toward the $1,800-$1,850 support zone before entering. The extreme fear reading suggests a broader opportunity is forming, but chasing coins that already bounced 20-30% is risky. Set alerts and be ready.
expert_reasoning: All swing candidates have significant issues. DEXE shows RSI 71 (overbought) despite a -53% 7d drop — conflicting signals suggest data lag and unreliable entry. LDO and ONDO have the best chart bias scores (+0.85 and +0.40) but both already ran 25-30% in 7 days with risk/reward ratios of only 0.38x to nearest resistance — terrible for new entries. BONK has a -2.0 chart bias (strongly bearish) with Three Black Crows, making it a falling knife. ETH at RSI 39 is the most promising setup but needs a pullback to support at $1,800 before the risk/reward justifies entry. Given high BTC dominance at 56.7% suppressing alts, and extreme fear sentiment that could worsen before improving, the disciplined move is to wait.
risk_factors: BTC dominance at 56.7% continues to siphon capital from altcoins — any alt entry faces headwinds
Extreme Fear at 25 could intensify further before a true reversal, potentially pushing alts lower
FTX $900M payout beginning could create selling pressure as creditors liquidate recovered assets
watch_list: ETH price action near $1,800-$1,850 support — a bounce with volume here is the ideal entry
Bitcoin ETF inflow streak — if this breaks, the entire market rally could stall
Fear & Greed index trajectory — a move below 20 would signal capitulation and stronger buy setup
market_context: Bitcoin is showing strength at $66.2K with a 5-day ETF inflow streak, but altcoins are lagging with BTC dominance at 56.7%. The market is in Extreme Fear (25/100), which historically precedes recoveries but timing is uncertain.
context_banner: Extreme Fear and strong BTC ETF flows are building a contrarian setup, but the best alt entries require patience for pullbacks to support.

key_signals.description: Extreme Fear at 25/100 with flat trend suggests contrarian buying opportunity is forming but not yet at capitulation levels.
ETH RSI at 39 is approaching oversold territory — our most reliable signal (83% win rate) — but needs a pullback to support for optimal entry.
Bitcoin ETFs posted 5 consecutive days of inflows (first since April), signaling sustained institutional demand supporting the BTC rally.
FTX begins $900M payout to creditors in 45 countries, creating potential near-term selling pressure as recovered funds may be liquidated.
Best candidates (LDO, ONDO) show bullish chart patterns but risk/reward ratios of 0.38x are far below the 2.0x minimum threshold for new entries.
